title: Andres Romero-Bravo
text:
Hi! I am a plant evolutionary biologist working on floral evolution, plant–pollinator interactions, and adaptation to environmental change.
Learn more about me · My research · CV

title: Andres Romero-Bravo
text:
Hola! I am Andrés, a plant evolutionary biologist focused on floral evolution, plant–pollinator interactions, and the evolvability of flowering plants. I am interested in studying the ecological and evolutionary mechanisms that determine the responses of flowering plants to environmental change.
In my research, I combine quantitative genetics, phenotypic plasticity, and field ecology to understand how reproductive and vegetative traits respond to changes in abiotic and biotic environments.
I hold a PhD from the University of Sussex, where I investigated floral plasticity, evolvability, and local adaptation under novel pollination environments. My work combines common-garden, transplant, and resurrection experiments with genomic and multivariate statistical approaches.
Beyond research, I am a co-founder and Secretary of Jóvenes por la Botánica Española (JxBE), a society for young botanists in Spain, where I am involved in the organisation of activities and events to promote botany among early-career researchers.
I am also committed to environmental education and science communication, drawing on my background in primary education to bridge academia and the wider public. Since 2018, I run La naturaleza al detalle, a nature photography blog where I combine images and educational text to share my passion for the natural world with a broad audience.
